title: Story content schema
description: The shared story shape across all subjects (spec c29): the unit of committed learning content. Language subjects use it for graded-reader ladders (beginner → advanced); culture-guide uses it for narrative scenarios. Stories live as committed content files in each subject repo and are embedded verbatim in `story read` payloads. Each subject repo validates its story files against this schema in CI.

id.description: Stable story id, unique within the subject (e.g. 'fr-beg-le-marche-du-samedi').

level.description: Coarse difficulty rung of the graded ladder.

level_detail.description: Finer grading: a CEFR tag ('A1', 'B2') for languages, 'scenario' for culture-guide narratives.

language.description: BCP-47 tag of the body text ('fr', 'es', 'en').

summary.description: One-line teaser, safe to show signed-out visitors in the catalog.

body.description: The story text. Markdown; paragraphs separated by blank lines.

glossary.description: Glossary/annotations: terms from the body the learner may not know. May be empty for advanced content.

note.description: Optional annotation: usage, grammar, or cultural context.

exercises.description: Comprehension exercises over the body; the driver runs and grades them, then records results.

audio.description: RESERVED for the spoken-practice follow-up (TTS/STT). Producers set null or omit until the audio pipeline exists; consumers must tolerate both.

source.description: Provenance for the batch-generation pipeline: how this story was produced and who reviewed it.

item_id.description: The curriculum item this exercise evidences — the id `record --item` expects.

choices.description: Required in spirit for multiple_choice; unused elsewhere.

answer.description: The expected answer, when one is checkable verbatim.

rubric.description: Grading guidance for open-ended exercises: what passes, what is partial.
